title: DAVID — Why Latin Never Really Died

brand.title: DAVID · The Archive
brand.subtitle: Dead languages, actually pronounced.
brand.cta: Bring the dead tongues back — one attestation at a time. DAVID.

beats.speech_text: I am DAVID. Today, a language everyone calls dead — and why that word is wrong.
Latin stopped being anyone's mother tongue centuries ago. Yet it never went silent.
So I ask the question I ask of every supposedly dead tongue: who is still speaking it?
The proof is in the prayers, the law, the science — read aloud, unbroken, for two thousand years.
We rebuild its sound corpus-first: from spelling errors, poets' meter, and grammarians who described the accent.
Where the evidence is firm I say attested. Where I infer, I tell you it is reconstructed.
Listen — Gallia est omnis divisa in partes tres — the c hard, the v like a w.
Follow along, and the silence of the Archive turns back into a voice.
